title: Efficient Median Calculation for Large Arrays

statement.markdown: Given a large array of integers, write a function to calculate and return the median of the array. The function should handle both even and odd-sized arrays, and it should be efficient for large arrays.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a list of integers.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a single number, the median of the input array.
statement.notes: The function should handle large arrays efficiently, and it should be able to handle both even and odd-sized arrays.

constraints.description: The size of the input array

examples.explanation: The median of this array is 3
The median of this array is 2.5
The median of this array is 12.5

hints.text: Use the QuickSelect algorithm to find the median in O(n) time

editorial.title: Efficient Median Calculation using QuickSelect
editorial.intuition: The QuickSelect algorithm is a variant of the QuickSort algorithm that can find the k-th smallest element in an array in O(n) time.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would be to sort the entire array and then find the median.
editorial.betterSolution: The QuickSelect algorithm can be used to find the median in O(n) time.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the QuickSelect algorithm to find the median in O(n) time.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The QuickSelect algorithm is correct because it always finds the k-th smallest element in an array in O(n) time.
editorial.dryRun: Let's consider an example array [1, 3, 5, 2, 4]. The median of this array is 3.

functionSignature.cpp: bool quickselect(int* arr, int n, int k);
functionSignature.java: boolean quickselect(int[] arr, int n, int k);
functionSignature.python: bool quickselect(self, arr: List[int], n: int, k: int) -> bool:
functionSignature.javascript: bool quickselect(arr: number[], n: number, k: number);
